_meta.description: Persistent living knowledge for the European Strategic Autonomy monitor (ESA). Carries forward KPI baselines, capability domains, programmes, stocks, dependencies, partner taxonomy, strategic-compass milestones, and windowed events across weekly issues.
_meta.prior_monitor_note: Prior persistent-state.json for this slug was populated with EGHTM-class data (hybrid-threat/FIMI monitor content). That content is preserved verbatim in the legacy_eghtm_data block below. ESA-spec module taxonomy is introduced on this baseline run as first-build for the ESA module schema. Consolidation gate #7 (array shrinkage) is waived per first-build proviso — no prior ESA-spec arrays existed.

_meta_preserved_from_prior.prior_description: Persistent living knowledge for the European Strategic Autonomy monitor (EGHTM). Carries forward KPI baselines, active elections, state capture cases, actor campaigns, EU legislation tracking, and timeline events across weekly issues.

structural_facts.fact: EU Defence Technological and Industrial Base (EDTIB) comprises approx. 3,000 firms across 27 member states; historically fragmented with 17 different types of main battle tanks vs 1 in US.

SAFE Regulation (Security Action for Europe) adopted 27 May 2025 as part of Readiness 2030 package; authorises €150B in EU-backed low-interest loans to member states for joint procurement of defence products. 65% EU-origin component requirement.
European Defence Industry Programme (EDIP) adopted by European Council 8 December 2025; budget €1.5B for 2025–2027; first dedicated EU industrial policy for defence. Calls opened March 2026.
European Defence Fund (EDF) 2021–2027 total commitment: €7.95B. Annual work programme for 2026 adopted December 2025 earmarks €1B across 31 call topics.
PESCO (Permanent Structured Cooperation) established 2017; 26 participating states (all EU except Malta); 75 projects active as of June 2025 after 6th wave of 11 new projects approved May 2025.
Act in Support of Ammunition Production (ASAP): €500M EU programme; target 2 million 155mm artillery shells annual capacity by end-2025. Rheinmetall projected 1.1–1.5M shells/year by 2027.

White Paper for European Defence – Readiness 2030 published 19 March 2025 by Commission and HR/VP Kallas. Defines Russia as 'fundamental threat'; identifies 7 priority capability areas: air/missile defence, artillery, ammunition/missiles, drones/counter-drones, strategic enablers, military mobility, AI/quantum/cyber/EW.

windowed_events.event: SAFE Regulation formally adopted by Council and Parliament (27 May 2025); €150B authorised for joint defence procurement.
Commission announces provisional SAFE allocation across 19 requesting member states. Poland top recipient (€43.7B); Romania (€16.7B); France and Hungary (€16.2B each); Italy (€14.9B).
European Council adopts EDIP (€1.5B 2025–2027), marking EU's first dedicated industrial defence policy.
Commission approves first SAFE disbursement wave for 8 member states (Belgium, Bulgaria, Denmark, Spain, Croatia, Cyprus, Portugal, Romania) — collectively ~€38B. Council approval sought.
Commission proposes €17.1B SAFE second wave for France (€15.09B) and Czech Republic (€2.06B).

Commission adopts EDIP 2026–2027 Work Programme: €700M+ for weapons production including counter-drone/missiles/ammo; €260M Ukraine Support Instrument; €325M for European Defence Projects of Common Interest (EDPCI); €240M joint procurement; €100M startup equity.

EDF 2025 calls results published: €1.07B awarded to 57 projects across 26 member states + Norway; 634 entities participating; 38%+ SMEs. Supports 4 flagships: European Drone Defence Initiative, Eastern Flank Watch, European Air Shield, European Space Shield.

PESCO 6th wave: 11 new projects approved including JEEWCI (electronic warfare, DE), modular directed-energy weapon (IT-ES), Quantum Enablers for Strategic Advantage QUEST (FI lead), Modular Seabed Vessel (FR lead). Total PESCO projects now 75.

US.qualitative: HIGH — F-35 software/weapons lock-in; US FMS EU purchases up 89% 2021–22; US intelligence databases for targeting. EDIS/White Paper explicitly targets reducing this dependency.

Russia.qualitative: Security threat driver rather than dependency. Russia identified as 'most significant and direct threat' to Euro-Atlantic security per NATO SG Annual Report 2025.

China.qualitative: MODERATE — EU pursuing strategic autonomy in defence; China not a primary defence-industrial dependency but competes in dual-use sectors.

structural_facts.fact: REPowerEU framework adopted 2022: target to end Russian energy dependency. EU gas dependency on Russia fell from 45% of imports (2021) to 12% (2025).

REPowerEU Gas Regulation (EU/261/2026) entered into force 3 February 2026: legally binding prohibition on Russian LNG by end-2026 (short-term contracts: ban from 25 April 2026; long-term LNG: 1 January 2027); Russian pipeline gas by 30 September 2027.

EU LNG import capacity increased by 76 bcm (2021–2025) to total 242 bcm/year through FSRU investments. Gas demand reduction ~19% vs pre-crisis reference period (equivalent ~80 bcm/year savings, Eurostat).

EU Hydrogen Strategy (2020): target 40 GW electrolyser capacity by 2030 for domestic renewable H2; REPowerEU target 10 Mt/year domestic production by 2030 (requires 65–140 GW electrolyser deployment). 6 GW by 2024 target substantially missed — <1 GW installed as of 2025.

EU ETS carbon price range €70–100/tonne (2026 reference). CBAM definitive regime commenced 1 January 2026 covering steel, cement, aluminium, electricity, fertilisers, hydrogen. Revenue estimate €2.1B/year by 2030.

windowed_events.event: EU political agreement on REPowerEU Gas Regulation — phase-out of Russian LNG by end-2026, Russian pipeline gas by end-Sept 2027.
EU Council formally adopts Regulation EU/261/2026 on phasing out Russian pipeline gas and LNG. Published in Official Journal 2 February 2026.
EU gas storage at <32% (18 Feb 2026) after unusually cold winter drawdown. Lowest since 2022 crisis baseline.
Commission publishes updated REPowerEU Gas Regulation implementation guidance; first implementation deadline: contracts concluded/amended after 17 June 2025 prohibited.
EU Gas Coordination Group confirms preparedness for summer refill season; storage ~29% full (8 Apr); target minimum 80% by 1 November 2026. Commission warns elevated prices for months ahead.
CBAM definitive regime enters into force. Importers must purchase certificates; 2.5% of full carbon cost payable in 2026, rising annually to 100% by 2034.

Six EU electrolyser manufacturers (ITM Power, John Cockerill, Nel, Sunfire, thyssenkrupp nucera, Topsoe) form Electrolysers for Europe (E4E) coalition; warn 6 GW 2024 target missed by wide margin (<1 GW installed). Call for urgent demand-side policy.

Russia.trajectory: Legally mandated phase-out: LNG by end-2026, pipeline gas by end-Sept 2027

US.qualitative: HIGH and rising — US LNG now ~60% of EU LNG imports (2025 estimate). Risk of lock-in as new long-term contracts signed post-Russian exit. Commissioner warned of gas price elevation.

OPEC_plus.qualitative: MODERATE — EU oil dependency on OPEC+ remains; Middle East LNG disruption (2026 regional conflict) tightened spot market for EU refill season.

structural_facts.fact: European Chips Act (Regulation EU 2023/1781) in force September 2023. Target: double EU share of global semiconductor production to 20% by 2030 from ~10% baseline. Total mobilisation target €43B public/private investment.

ESMC (European Semiconductor Manufacturing Company): TSMC + Bosch + Infineon + NXP joint venture, Dresden Germany. 300mm fab, ~2nm-class processes. €5B German state aid approved early 2025 by Commission. First production targeted late 2020s.
Infineon MEGAFAB-DD Dresden: €3.5B total investment; €920M German state aid approved by Commission. Additional flexible front-end fab capacity for industrial/automotive/consumer segments.
All 27 EU member states signed joint declaration Sept 29 2025 calling for 'revised and forward-looking Chips Act'. Commission work programme 2026 included Chips Act 2.0 proposal for Q1 2026.

windowed_events.event: All 27 EU member states sign declaration calling for revised Chips Act (Chips Act 2.0). Focus on bridging lab-to-fab gap and strengthening coordination.
Commission announces milestone in semiconductor manufacturing capacity under Chips Act: first-of-a-kind Integrated Production Facilities and Open EU Foundries milestones reached.
European Commission approves €5B German state aid for ESMC fab (TSMC/Bosch/Infineon/NXP joint venture, Dresden). Part of Chips Act Pillar 2 framework.
Commission approves €920M German state aid for Infineon MEGAFAB-DD in Dresden (€3.5B total investment). Additional front-end fab capacity for industrial/automotive/consumer applications.
All 27 EU member states sign joint declaration calling for Chips Act 2.0 focused on bridging lab-to-fab gap. Commission 2026 Work Programme includes Chips Act 2.0 proposal in Q1 2026 pipeline.
European Chips Act (Regulation EU 2023/1781) enters into force. Framework for Integrated Production Facilities and Open EU Foundries established. State aid for first-of-a-kind fabs permitted.

US.type: EDA tools / IP / advanced chip design
US.qualitative: HIGH — EU relies on US EDA (Synopsys, Cadence, Mentor) and US IP cores. Chips Act targets fab capacity but not design-tool dependency.

Taiwan.qualitative: HIGH — TSMC still dominates sub-5nm processes. ESMC Dresden will produce 'next generation' but timeline extends to late 2020s. 20% global share target by 2030 considered ambitious.

China.qualitative: MODERATE as direct chip supplier; HIGH via rare-earth inputs for compound semiconductors.

structural_facts.fact: Critical Raw Materials Act (CRMA) entered into force 23 May 2024. Identifies 34 critical and 17 strategic raw materials. Sets 2030 benchmarks: 10% EU extraction, 40% EU processing, 25% EU recycling; no single third country >65% of any strategic material at any processing stage.

China accounts for ~70% of EU rare-earth imports and ~91–98% of global rare-earth refining. EU has negligible processing capacity. ECB estimates 98% of EU magnesium from China. Meaningful diversification not expected before 2030s.

China enacted rare-earth export restriction tightening (Oct 2024) amid US trade war; restrictions partially suspended for one year but licenses now required for certain exports. Triggered supply-chain turmoil in EU and global markets.

windowed_events.event: CRMA enters into force. Member states must begin annual strategic stockpile reporting from 2026.

EU presents updated rare-earth diversification plan; proposes joint purchasing mechanism (COVID-vaccine model), European Centre for Critical Raw Materials, boosted local production/recycling, partnership with AU/BR/ZA.

EU-US nearing critical minerals coordination pact: draft action plan includes minimum pricing mechanisms, joint projects, coordinated response to supply disruptions. Parallel Japan and Australia involvement.

China enacts new rare-earth export restriction tightening amid US trade war. EU and global supply chains face turmoil. ECB assesses 98% EU magnesium supply from China as critical vulnerability. CRMA implementation response accelerated.

EU-US-Japan ministerial meeting commits to accelerate critical minerals cooperation; intends action plans as groundwork for binding plurilateral critical minerals trade framework. USTR statement issued simultaneously.

CRMA first anniversary: Commission identifies 'large companies' using strategic raw materials per Article 9 requirements. Companies must complete supply chain risk assessments. Annual strategic stockpile reporting framework established for 2026 first reports.

China.share: ~70% rare-earth imports; ~91% global refining; 97-98% EU magnesium supply
China.trajectory: CRMA targets 65% cap per material per country by 2030; realistically not achievable before 2030s per ECB/academic assessment

Others.qualitative: MODERATE — geographic diversification strategy underway via CRMA strategic partnerships

structural_facts.fact: Digital Markets Act (DMA) in force May 2023; compliance required from March 2024. Six gatekeepers designated Sept 2023: Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, ByteDance, Meta, Microsoft. 22 core platform services designated. Fines up to 10% worldwide turnover; 20% for repeat.

EU Data Act (Regulation 2023/2854): in force 11 January 2024; applicable 12 September 2025 (core provisions); cloud switching rights active. Enhanced interoperability requirements for cloud services: September 2026. Complete ban on cloud-switching charges: January 2027.

EU AI Act (Regulation EU 2024/1689) entered into force 1 August 2024. Full applicability 2 August 2026. Prohibited AI practices (Article 5) became effective 2 February 2025. High-risk AI system requirements applicable from August 2026. Fines up to 7% worldwide turnover.

GAIA-X: launched 2019/2020; federated European cloud data infrastructure initiative. By early 2026: 380+ member organisations, 12 operational data spaces, GXFS (Federation Services) in production, Trust Framework v22.10 finalised. No European hyperscaler created; remains standards/governance framework.

US cloud hyperscalers (AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud) hold ~70%+ of EU cloud infrastructure market. EU Digital Europe Programme (2021–2027) total €8.2B for digital including cloud/data infrastructure. IPCEI CIS (cloud/edge): €1.4B state aid approved Dec 2023 for FR/DE/IT/ES.

windowed_events.event: EU AI Act prohibited practices (Article 5, 8 categories) become legally binding. First major enforcement milestone.
EU Data Act core provisions applicable: data access rights for connected product users active; cloud-switching provisions in force; enforcement by national authorities ramping up.
Commission introduces Digital Omnibus Package: proposes consolidating Data Act, AI Act, GDPR amendments. Raises concern among digital sovereignty advocates about weakening protections.
All six DMA-designated gatekeepers (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, ByteDance, Meta, Microsoft) submit updated compliance reports and audited consumer profiling reports. Commission analysis ongoing.

IPCEI CIS (Cloud Infrastructure and Services) approved by Commission: €1.4B state aid from FR/DE/IT/ES for 12 sovereign cloud and edge computing projects. Includes GAIA-X infrastructure development components.

[UPCOMING] EU Data Act enhanced cloud interoperability requirements apply from 12 September 2026: enhanced switching requirements for cloud services take effect; major compliance milestone for cloud-switching market.

US.type: cloud infrastructure / AI platforms / EDA / operating systems
US.share: ~70%+ EU cloud market held by US hyperscalers
US.qualitative: HIGH — Structural dependency deepening despite GAIA-X. US executive pressure on GDPR/DSA enforcement (US-003 campaign) adds policy risk layer.

China.qualitative: MODERATE — Huawei/ZTE 5G phase-out legislation proposed Jan 2026 (LEG-003 in legacy tracker). Chinese hardware in telecom infrastructure remains.

structural_facts.fact: IRIS² (Infrastructure for Resilience, Interconnectivity and Security by Satellite): EU sovereign secure connectivity constellation. Concession contract signed 2024 with SpaceRISE consortium. 290+ LEO satellites (264 at 1,200km + 10–20 test satellites at <750km). Full governmental services by 2030; initial services via GOVSATCOM pooling from 2025.

EU Space Programme includes Galileo (GNSS), Copernicus (Earth observation), GOVSATCOM, and IRIS². Galileo declared fully operational with 30+ MEO satellites. Galileo OSNMA (Open Service Navigation Message Authentication) service launched to counter spoofing; Galileo PRS (Public Regulated Service) for military/security use imminent as of 2026.

EU Space programme 2021–2027 budget: €14.88B covering Galileo/EGNOS, Copernicus, SSA/Govsatcom, IRIS². EUSPA (EU Agency for the Space Programme) coordinates security accreditation and operational service provision. EU space sector directly employs ~230,000 people; downstream applications worth ~€250B GVA.

windowed_events.event: IRIS² consortium books additional 340 OneWeb satellites (Jan 2026) from Airbus to refurbish existing constellation as bridge capacity until IRIS² LEO shell launches (target 2029). First IRIS² satellites will omit 7nm chips due to supply-chain readiness — to be introduced 2031+.

EUSPA 2025 retrospect confirms EU Space Programme secured security/defence focus during Polish Presidency: Galileo integrated with Frontex border surveillance, OSNMA operationally demonstrated in Baltic region.

European Space Conference 2026: ESA Director-General confirms Galileo PRS imminent for military use; LEO augmentation layer planned; EU reaffirms sovereign PNT capability as security-critical. Galileo marks 10 years of operational service.

IRIS² programme update: SpaceRISE consortium confirms incremental deployment approach; Hispasat leads low-LEO test shell (<750km, 10–20 satellites); Eutelsat leads main 264-satellite shell at 1,200km. Overlap period with OneWeb planned.
European Space Conference 2025 (Brussels): Galileo, Copernicus, SSA, GOVSATCOM acceleration confirmed; EU positions space as key component of security and defence pillar per Readiness 2030.

US.type: GPS backup / launch services / satellite components
US.qualitative: MODERATE — Galileo provides EU sovereign GNSS but US GPS still widely used in dual-constellation mode. SpaceX launch dependency reduced by Ariane 6 restoration.

structural_facts.fact: EU Cybersecurity Act amendment (Cybersecurity Act 2.0) proposed January 2026: mandates Huawei/ZTE phase-out from critical EU telecoms infrastructure; updates ENISA role; expands incident reporting; adds ransomware reporting obligations under revised NIS2.

EU 5G Toolbox (2020): member-state-led; implementation uneven. Multiple MS still have Huawei/ZTE in RAN. Swedish, Estonian, Romanian exclusions most advanced. Proposed Cybersecurity Act 2.0 would make exclusion EU-mandatory. Phase-out costs estimated at tens of billions EUR across EU.

NIS2 Directive transposition: 4 MS met October 2024 deadline; infringement procedures opened against 23 MS in November 2024. By March 2026: 22 of 27 MS have transposed NIS2. Germany published implementation law December 2025; registration deadline March 6 2026. 2026 set to be first year of enforcement actions.

windowed_events.event: Commission proposes Cybersecurity Act 2.0 and NIS2 amendments: mandates Huawei/ZTE phase-out; adds ransomware reporting obligations; updates ENISA mandate; adjusts certification frameworks.
Commission opens infringement procedures against 23 of 27 member states for missing NIS2 transposition deadline (17 Oct 2024). Only 4 MS (CZ, HR, IT, LV early movers) on time.
Commission publishes NIS2 reform proposal details: new ransomware reporting, expanded scope, fragmented national implementation highlighted as compliance challenge. 22/27 MS transposed by March 2026.
Germany publishes NIS2 implementation law (5 December 2025); covers ~30,000 entities in 18 sectors. One of largest national implementations in EU.
2026 NIS2 enforcement era begins: 2026 first year of enforcement actions expected per Commission and legal analysis. Compliance fragmentation across MS creates single-market tension.

China.qualitative: HIGH in several member states — Huawei/ZTE installed base in RAN. Phase-out costs estimated at tens of billions EUR across EU. Legislation pending.

structural_facts.fact: EU AI Act is world's first comprehensive AI regulation. Prohibitions (Article 5) effective Feb 2025; full applicability August 2026. High-risk AI categories include biometric, critical infrastructure, education, employment, public services AI. GPAI model rules apply from August 2025.

EU lacks sovereign large AI compute infrastructure at scale comparable to US hyperscalers. EuroHPC Joint Undertaking operates 8 EU supercomputers. AI Factories programme: 6 AI Factories launched October 2025 in CZ, LT, NL, RO, ES, PL. Targeted for AI training workloads.

AI Gigafactories programme (Regulation EU 2026/150, Jan 2026): channels up to €4.12B from Horizon Europe + Digital Europe + CEF-Digital into EuroHPC JU. EU AI Strategy mobilises ~€1B additional. Total EU sovereign AI infrastructure investment envelope ~€5.1B by 2030.

windowed_events.event: EU AI Act prohibited AI practices enter into legal effect. 8 prohibited categories active, including subliminal manipulation, social scoring, mass facial recognition database creation, real-time biometric ID for law enforcement.
EuroHPC JU announces launch of 6 AI Factories (CZ, LT, NL, RO, ES, PL) — first EU sovereign AI compute facilities for AI training workloads. Deployment target end-2026.
Council Regulation 2026/150 enters into force: channels up to €4.12B to EuroHPC JU for AI Gigafactory construction. Legal basis for EU sovereign large-scale AI compute established.
Digital Omnibus Package IMCO Committee debate: concerns raised over AI Act/GDPR weakening under US diplomatic pressure. US Section 301 investigation targets EU digital regulations.
Commission announces €307M in AI funding via Horizon Europe Cluster 4 (Digital/Industry/Space): €221.8M across 15 calls for trustworthy AI, strategic autonomy AI, and innovative data use.

US.qualitative: VERY HIGH — NVIDIA GPU monopoly on training; US hyperscalers host EU AI workloads; US foundation models (GPT, Gemini, Claude) dominate EU enterprise deployment. EU AI Act applies to non-EU providers serving EU market but enforcement of US firms uncertain.

structural_facts.fact: NIS2 Directive (EU 2022/2555) transposition deadline 17 October 2024; substantially expands scope to 18 sectors and 160,000+ entities. ENISA coordinates EU cybersecurity; ECCC (European Cybersecurity Competence Centre, Bucharest) operational. 22/27 MS transposed by March 2026.

EU Cyber Diplomacy Toolbox (2017/2023 updated) enables collective attribution and response to state-sponsored cyberattacks. EU Cyber Diplomacy framework includes asset-freeze/travel-ban measures for attackers. Council adopted hybrid threat deterrence conclusions April 2026 formalising cyber diplomacy.

EU Cybersecurity Act 2.0 (proposed Jan 2026) updates ENISA mandate; adjusts cybersecurity certification frameworks; adds ransomware reporting under NIS2 reform. 2026 designated as first year of active NIS2 enforcement by Commission.

windowed_events.event: EU Council adopts comprehensive hybrid threat deterrence conclusions; formally integrates hybrid toolbox, NIS2 implementation acceleration, and cyber diplomacy framework.

Commission opens infringement procedures against 23 of 27 EU member states for failing to transpose NIS2 by October 2024 deadline. Only 4 MS compliant at deadline. Largest single infringement action in EU cybersecurity history.
Commission proposes NIS2 amendments: ransomware reporting obligations, expanded scope, updated representative appointment requirements. Part of broader Cybersecurity Act 2.0 legislative package.
Germany publishes NIS2 national implementation (NIS2UmsuCG): covers ~30,000 entities; registration deadline 6 March 2026. One of the most expansive national NIS2 transpositions.

150+ Russian hybrid attacks recorded across Europe since 2022 per tracking data; attacks quadrupled 2023–2024 per Robert Schuman Foundation study (40 arson plots DE/PL). EU hybrid/cyber threat environment assessed at peak since 2022 invasion.

US.qualitative: HIGH — EU relies on US SIGINT/HUMINT sharing (Five Eyes adjacent); US cybersecurity vendors (CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, Microsoft Defender) dominant in EU enterprise.

structural_facts.fact: IPCEI Health (Important Project of Common European Interest) in health data/biotech approved 2023 involving multiple MS. EMA (European Medicines Agency, Amsterdam) provides EU-level regulatory coordination. EU strategic pharmaceutical manufacturing gap identified as a critical dependency post-COVID.

EU imports ~60–80% of active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) from China and India. EU Critical Medicines Alliance and European Health Emergency Preparedness and Response Authority (HERA) established to address supply vulnerability. EU Pharmaceutical Strategy (2020) targets reshoring critical API production.

HERA (Health Emergency Preparedness and Response Authority) operational since 2021; manages EU strategic medical countermeasure stockpiles, vaccine advance purchase, and biodefence preparedness. Budget €6B for 2022–2027.

windowed_events.event: EU activates first IPI measure (medical devices against China): blocks Chinese medical device producers from EU public procurement contracts ≥€5M from June 30 2025. Directly addresses API/medical device strategic dependency. China reciprocates against EU medical device makers in CN market.
EDF 2025 results include EU Alliance for Defence Medical Countermeasures under EDF 2026 Work Programme (€60M+ allocation). Demonstrates defence-biotech convergence in EDTIB context.

European Commission proposes Critical Medicines Act (Regulation): targets reshoring EU API and generic medicine manufacturing, introduces 'strategic projects' designation for EU pharma production sites, enables EU-wide procurement coordination for >50 critical medicines. Parliament adopts position Jan 20 2026 (503-57). Addresses ~60-80% API import dependency on China/India.

European Commission publishes draft European Biotech Act (250+ pages): proposes 'Strategic Health Biotechnology Projects' designation, 12-month SPC extension for qualifying biotech/ATMP products, single regulatory pathway for ATMPs, and IPCEI-style support for biotech industrial clusters. Responds to competitive pressure from US and China biotech investment. Complements Critical Medicines Act.

European Shortages Monitoring Platform (ESMP) launched (target Feb 2025) by EMA under Regulation 2022/123: centralises shortage reporting and supply/demand data across all EU member states. PGEU 2025 survey (Jan-Feb 2026) finds shortages persist in 96% of EU/EFTA countries, >600 medicines in shortage in >1/3 of countries including insulins, antibiotics, and cardiovascular drugs — confirming structural vulnerability.

China.qualitative: HIGH — EU imports ~60–80% of active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) from China and India. Strategic medicines dependency identified but not yet systematically addressed.

capability_programmes.name: FCAS — Future Combat Air System
capability_programmes.current_phase: Phase 1B demonstrator development (signed Dec 2022); Phase 2 contract negotiation ongoing as of Q1 2026
capability_programmes.objective: 6th-generation fighter + loyal-wingman drones + combat cloud; entry into service ~2040

structural_facts.fact: FCAS Phase 1A launched Feb 2020 (Dassault, Airbus, MTU, Safran, MBDA, Thales). Phase 1B signed Dec 2022; Indra €600M contract to end-2025. Demonstrator test flight target ~2027; operational ~2040.

windowed_events.event: India expresses interest in joining FCAS programme (Feb 2026); Indian MoD includes FCAS alongside GCAP as candidate 6th-gen programmes for Indian Air Force consideration.

Dassault CEO Trappier gives deadline for resolving FCAS leadership structure. Disputes over IP and flight-control software systems remain unresolved. Described as existential decision point for programme.

capability_programmes.name: MGCS — Main Ground Combat System
capability_programmes.budget_envelope: Estimate €100B+ lifecycle; MGCS Project Company (MPC) established April 2025
capability_programmes.current_phase: Technology Demonstration Phase (TDP); programme delayed past 2040; interim 'Leopard 3' under cartel approval
capability_programmes.objective: Replace Leopard 2 and Leclerc main battle tanks; current target ~2045

structural_facts.fact: MGCS Project Company (MPC) established Cologne April 2025: KNDS Deutschland, KNDS France, Rheinmetall, Thales as joint-venture prime. Programme behind schedule due to Franco-German political disputes.

windowed_events.event: MGCS programme slips past 2040 per meta-defense.fr analysis. France Leclerc XLR upgrade limited to 200 units without engine repowering. German Federal Cartel Office approves KNDS/Rheinmetall interim 'Leopard 3' development.
KNDS CEO confirms IPO scheduled for 2026 independent of MGCS delays. MGCS target now 2045.

capability_programmes.name: IRIS² — EU Sovereign Satellite Constellation
lead_nations: EU Commission + SpaceRISE consortium (SES, Eutelsat, Hispasat)
capability_programmes.current_phase: Industrial design / early procurement; first launches target 2029; full capability 2030
capability_programmes.objective: Sovereign EU secure governmental connectivity (GovSatCom) + commercial broadband; 290+ LEO/MEO/GEO satellites

structural_facts.fact: IRIS² concession contract signed 2024. Incremental deployment: initial governmental services via GOVSATCOM pooling 2025; own infrastructure first launches ~2029; full capability by 2030.

windowed_events.event: Eutelsat orders additional 340 OneWeb satellites from Airbus as bridge capacity. IRIS² first satellites will lack 7nm chips (deferred to 2031+ generation). Compromise adopted to achieve 2029 first-launch target.

capability_programmes.name: EDF — European Defence Fund
capability_programmes.current_phase: Execution — 5th round of calls completed April 2026; 57 projects selected from 410 proposals
capability_programmes.objective: Collaborative R&D/capability development for EDTIB; supports 4 flagships from 2026: European Drone Defence Initiative, Eastern Flank Watch, European Air Shield, European Space Shield

structural_facts.fact: EDF 2025 calls (5th round): 410 proposals submitted (37% increase YoY); 57 projects selected for €1.07B. 634 entities from 26 EU member states + Norway; >38% SMEs.

windowed_events.event: Commission adopts EDF 2026 Annual Work Programme: €1B across 31 call topics in 7 thematic + 3 non-thematic calls. Focus on quantum-secured networks, EW, multi-domain ops cloud, hypersonic glide vehicle countermeasures.
EDF 2025 call results published: 57 projects, €1.07B awarded. Record proposals submitted. 4 EU defence flagships formally supported.

capability_stocks.context: Post-cold-winter drawdown from ~80% (Nov 2025). Summer refill season complicated by Middle East LNG competition and Russian gas phase-out timeline.

capability_stocks.value: ~1–1.5M shells/year (current); target 2M/year by end-2025 under ASAP
capability_stocks.context: ASAP programme (€500M) targeting 2M/year capacity. Rheinmetall (dominant producer) projecting 1.1–1.5M by 2027. Target partially missed. European production tripled since 2022.

capability_stocks.metric: EU share of global semiconductor production
capability_stocks.context: ESMC and Infineon MEGAFAB-DD Dresden under construction. Gap between current 10% and 20% target considered challenging without additional fab investments.

capability_stocks.metric: Installed EU renewable hydrogen electrolyser capacity
capability_stocks.target: 6 GW by 2024 (missed); 40 GW by 2030 (Hydrogen Strategy); 10 Mt/year domestic by 2030 (REPowerEU)
capability_stocks.context: Manufacturing capacity ~9–13 GW/year available but deployment severely lags. Only 0.9 Mt under binding supply contracts (4.5% of REPowerEU 2030 target). E4E coalition warning of policy gap.

capability_stocks.metric: EU sovereign cloud share of EU cloud market
capability_stocks.target: No formal EU-wide target; France Cloud de Confiance targets 100% government sovereign cloud
capability_stocks.context: GAIA-X 380+ members, 12 operational data spaces but no hyperscaler-equivalent. IPCEI CIS (€1.4B) funding 12 projects in FR/DE/IT/ES. Gap between regulatory ambition and infrastructure reality.

capability_stocks.metric: EU total defence spending (all member states combined)
capability_stocks.context: All 32 NATO allies met 2% GDP target for first time in 2025. EU+Canada total $574B; 20% real-term increase vs 2024. Poland 4.48%, Lithuania 4%, Latvia 3.73% as top spenders. New Hague Summit commitment: 5% by 2035 (3.5% core defence + 1.5% security-adjacent).

dependency_matrix.share_or_qualitative: 12% of EU gas imports (2025); down from 45% (2021)
dependency_matrix.trajectory: Legally mandated phase-out 2026–2027 under Regulation EU/261/2026

dependency_matrix.share_or_qualitative: ~60% of EU LNG imports (2025 estimate); lock-in risk via long-term contracts
dependency_matrix.trajectory: Growing as Russian gas exits; new long-term US LNG contracts signed 2022–2025

dependency_matrix.share_or_qualitative: ~70% rare-earth imports; ~91% global refining; 97-98% magnesium supply
dependency_matrix.trajectory: CRMA targets 65% cap per country by 2030; realistically >2030 for material reduction

dependency_matrix.share_or_qualitative: TSMC ~90%+ of sub-5nm production globally; US EDA (Synopsys/Cadence) ~100% of chip design tooling
dependency_matrix.trajectory: ESMC Dresden targeted for late 2020s. Chips Act 2.0 in preparation.

dependency_matrix.share_or_qualitative: ~70%+ EU cloud market; ~100% of leading LLM AI models; dominant OS/productivity stack
dependency_matrix.trajectory: Structural; GAIA-X / Digital Europe Programme reduce at margins. Digital Omnibus risks weakening regulatory moat.

dependency_matrix.share_or_qualitative: F-35 (~600+ EU aircraft ordered); US FMS up 89% 2021–22; US databases for targeting. SAFE/EDIP require 65% EU content — gradually reducing
dependency_matrix.trajectory: White Paper / EDIS explicitly targets reduction; multi-year horizon

dependency_matrix.share_or_qualitative: Present in multiple EU member states; exact share varies by MS; phase-out legislated
dependency_matrix.trajectory: Proposed mandatory phase-out via Cybersecurity Act amendment (2026); cost tens of billions across EU

dependency_matrix.type: manufactured goods / EV supply chain / industrial inputs
dependency_matrix.share_or_qualitative: EU-China trade: imports €559.4B in 2025 (+6.4% YoY per Eurostat Mar 2026); EU trade deficit with China remains large
dependency_matrix.trajectory: CBAM, FSR, IPI, EV tariffs all targeting dependency reduction and level-playing-field

NATO.eu_overlap: 23 of 27 EU member states are NATO members (exceptions: AT, CY, IE, MT)
NATO.status: All 32 allies met 2% GDP target for first time in 2025. New Hague Summit (2025) commitment: 5% GDP by 2035 (3.5% core defence + 1.5% defence-adjacent). Next summit: Ankara, July 2026.

Bucharest_Nine.status: ACTIVE — coordinates eastern flank NATO/EU defence posture. Highest per-capita defence spenders (PL 4.48%, LT 4%, LV 3.73%). Focal point for eastern border reinforcement.

Nordic_Baltic_Eight.status: ACTIVE — Deepened integration post-Finland/Sweden NATO accession (2023/2024). Nordic-Baltic cooperation intensified on air defence, seabed security, military mobility.

Three_Seas_Initiative.status: ACTIVE — Focus on energy, transport, digital infrastructure interconnection from Baltic to Adriatic/Black Seas. Three Seas Fund operational.

Med_Seven.status: ACTIVE — southern neighbourhood focus: migration, energy, Mediterranean security. France leads with strongest Mediterranean naval/political presence.

AUKUS.eu_relationship: Non-member; France excluded following AUKUS submarine contract dispute (2021). Pillar II technology cooperation (AI, quantum, hypersonics) interests some EU members bilaterally. UK post-Brexit security partner (UK-EU Security and Defence Partnership under development).
AUKUS.status: External — limited EU institutional engagement. Individual member state bilateral cooperation (e.g. DE with AU on critical minerals).

Indo_Pacific_Tilt.eu_strategy: EU Indo-Pacific Strategy (2021); strategic partnerships with JP, KR, AU, IN, ASEAN. Growing critical minerals cooperation (AU, ZA, BR). Semiconductors: partnership with JP (NEDO) and KR (Samsung, SK Hynix).
Indo_Pacific_Tilt.status: ACTIVE but limited institutionalisation relative to NATO/eastern flank focus

strategic_compass_milestones.event: EU Strategic Compass adopted by European Council. Four pillars: Act, Secure, Invest, Partner. Key targets: EU Rapid Deployment Capacity (RDC) 5,000 troops by 2025; annual live exercise from 2023; Article 44 TEU coalitions of willing.
EU Rapid Deployment Capacity (RDC) declared fully operational — 5,000 troops deployable across land, air, maritime, space, cyber components. Achieved 2025 target from Strategic Compass.

White Paper for European Defence – Readiness 2030 published. ReArm Europe package presented: €150B SAFE instrument + Stability and Growth Pact defence exemption. Identifies 7 priority capability areas. EU mobilisation envelope up to €800B.

European Defence Industrial Programme (EDIP) formally adopted by European Council: €1.5B for 2025–2027; first dedicated EU industrial defence policy. Requires 65% EU component content for funded projects.
First SAFE disbursements: Commission approves defence plans for 8 member states (~€38B combined). Marks first actual money flow under €150B programme.
All 32 NATO allies meet 2% GDP defence target for first time — milestone for collective European defence commitment. Europe+Canada: $574B total; 20% real increase vs 2024.
EDIP 2026–2027 Work Programme adopted; first calls for proposals opened. €1.5B total; €700M+ weapons production; €260M Ukraine Support Instrument.

PESCO 6th wave: 11 new projects approved (EW doctrine, quantum, directed energy, seabed vessels). Phase 2026–2030 planning underway to address persistent shortfalls beyond patching. Total now 75 projects.

CBAM.name: Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism
CBAM.status: Definitive regime in force from 1 January 2026
CBAM.scope: Steel, cement, aluminium, electricity, fertilisers, hydrogen
CBAM.phase_in: 2.5% of full carbon cost in 2026; ramps to 100% by 2034 as free ETS allowances phase out

FSR.name: Foreign Subsidies Regulation
FSR.status: In force 12 July 2023; substantive assessment guidelines adopted 9 January 2026
FSR.scope: M&A concentrations and public procurement bids by third-country-subsidised entities
FSR.key_cases: e&/PPF (UAE, conditionally cleared Sep 2024); CRRC Qingdao (CN, withdrew Mar 2024); ENEVO/LONGi and Shanghai Electric (CN, withdrew Jun 2024)

IPI.name: International Procurement Instrument
IPI.scope: Chinese medical devices excluded from EU public procurement (CPV 33100000–33199000) for contracts ≥€5M. Applies until June 2030. China reciprocated — EU operators excluded from CN medical device procurement >RMB 45M.

EV_tariffs.name: Anti-subsidy duties on Chinese BEVs
EV_tariffs.status: Definitive duties in force from October 2024; price-undertaking negotiation underway Jan 2026
EV_tariffs.scope: BYD: 17%; Geely: 18.8%; SAIC: 35.3% (plus existing 10% MFN duty)
EV_tariffs.recent_development: EU-China framework agreement on minimum price undertaking reportedly agreed Jan 2026; EU guidance published on 'price undertakings' mechanism as alternative to direct tariff payment

ukraine.accession_status: Candidate country; accession negotiations formally launched 25 June 2024 (first IGC); screening completed; all 6 clusters ready to open but blocked by Hungary veto
ukraine.current_blocker: Hungary veto on formal cluster opening; technical guidance provided to UA+MD on 17 March 2026 to continue work across all 6 clusters pending political conditions
ukraine.ukraine_goal: Open all 6 clusters and close at least 1 chapter by end-2026; accession treaty drafting in 2027; ratification 2028+. Confirmed at April 23 2026 EU Summit in Cyprus.
ukraine.eu_support_total: €194.9B total EU support to Ukraine (confirmed European Council March 2026)

moldova.accession_status: Candidate country; accession negotiations formally launched 25 June 2024; screening completed September 2025; awaiting formal cluster opening
moldova.recent_milestone: Commission delivered second €189M payment under Growth Plan to Moldova (March 2026)

georgia.accession_status: Candidate status granted Dec 2023; accession negotiations 'de-facto halted' after Oct 2024 election returned GD government; laws criminalising foreign grants passed March 2026

western_balkans.frontrunners: Montenegro (negotiations since 2012; could close by end-2026 per EU Commissioner Kos); Albania (could close 2027); North Macedonia + Serbia (active but slower); Bosnia-Herzegovina + Kosovo (early stage)
western_balkans.growth_plan: Reform and Growth Facility for Western Balkans: €6B (€2B grants + €4B concessional loans) for 2024–2027. Serbia first release approved January 2026. Bosnia-Herzegovina Reform Agenda approved November 2025.
western_balkans.geopolitical_note: Serbia in strategic limbo oscillating between EU, Russia, China. Accelerated enlargement for Montenegro/Albania as EU security investment.

energy.note: Russian gas 45%→12%, but US LNG at ~60%; storage at 29% post-winter; refill season under pressure

defence.note: Up from 28%; SAFE €22.5B first tranche + €17.1B second wave; +20% spending; EDIP launched; RDC operational

technology.note: US cloud 70%+; Digital Omnibus weakening protections; AI Act in effect but US AI dominant

finance.note: Euro 20.3% of reserves; Capital Markets Union progress slow

materials.note: 97-98% Mg from China; RE restrictions partially suspended; CRMA 2030 targets at risk; EU-US minerals pact in negotiation

institutions.note: QMV blocked on Ukraine accession; Hungary veto operative; ReArm Europe rhetoric ahead of contracts

active_elections.key_facts: Tisza 47.8% vs Fidesz 40.5% (PolitPro aggregate Mar 31); Republikon Mar 26: Tisza 49 vs Fidesz 40; Vance visit announced 'early April'; €90B Ukraine loan blocked by Orbán at European Council; Issue 3 (Apr 8): 4 days to election; PolitPro aggregate Tisza 47.8% vs Fidesz 40.5%; RU/US/IL triple interference active; CRITICAL monitoring window

active_elections.name: Slovenia Parliamentary Election (occurred)
active_elections.key_facts: GS 29 seats + allies = 40; SDS 28 + allies = 43. Neither at 46. Parliament inaugurates 10 April. SDS early voting complaints rejected. Seven parties in parliament.

version_history.reason: Euronews/Reuters reporting on coalition talks and timeline

eu_legislation_tracker.name: SAFE Regulation (€150B defence loans)
eu_legislation_tracker.status: First disbursements Jan 2026; second wave proposed Mar 2026

eu_legislation_tracker.name: CBAM (Carbon Border Adjustment)
eu_legislation_tracker.status: Definitive regime in force from 1 Jan 2026

eu_legislation_tracker.name: REPowerEU Gas Regulation (EU/261/2026)
eu_legislation_tracker.status: In force 3 February 2026; short-term LNG ban from 25 April 2026

eu_legislation_tracker.name: EU AI Act
eu_legislation_tracker.status: Prohibited practices in force 2 Feb 2025; full applicability 2 Aug 2026

timeline_events.event: EU sanctions on Russia renewed to September 2026
EU Council adopts hybrid threat counter-conclusions
EEAS FIMI Explorer launched; 4th FIMI Report published
V-Dem 2026 Democracy Report released
Czech Pardubice drone warehouse arson
CPAC Hungary 2026 — 45 speakers, 51 countries
Slovenia parliamentary election — razor-thin result
ISW confirms Russia spring-summer offensive begun
Patriots Grand Assembly in Budapest — Le Pen, Salvini, Wilders
3 arrested on terrorism charges in Czech Pardubice case
BBC 'The Price of the Vote' Hungary voter intimidation documentary
NATO Annual Report: European allies +20% defence spending
Russia gasoline export ban directive (Apr-Jul 2026) from Deputy PM Novak
Eurostat: EU 2025 trade data — China imports €559.4B (+6.4% YoY)
Commission proposes €17.1B SAFE second wave (FR €15.09B + CZ €2.06B)
Zelensky proposes Easter ceasefire — Kremlin dismisses
ISW: Russian milbloggers admit offensive stalling; Duma questions 2026 objectives
Czech intelligence probes Russian false-flag scenario in Pardubice arson
Slovenia PM Golob launches broad coalition talks
European Council: €194.9B Ukraine support confirmed; €90B loan blocked by Orbán; 20th sanctions package prepared
EU Council adopts comprehensive hybrid threat deterrence conclusions — hybrid toolbox, NIS2, cyber diplomacy formalised
ECFR publishes 'From Shield to Sword: Europe's Offensive Strategy for the Hybrid Age'
Robert Schuman Foundation study: 40 Russia-linked arson plots Germany/Poland 2018-2025 (attacks quadrupled 2023-2024)
Russian FIMI campaign amplifies AfD narratives in German elections.
Belarusian drone probes target Polish rail infrastructure.
Chinese opaque funding to pro-BRI Italian think tanks.
EU-US nearing critical minerals coordination pact; draft action plan under review (Bloomberg).
EDF 2025 results: €1.07B awarded to 57 projects; 634 entities; 4 defence flagships supported.
EU Summit Cyprus: EU leaders agree Ukraine accession cluster opening in weeks/months; first cluster opening expected May 2026.

P1_defence_industrial.note: Rhetoric > capability. Score contracts signed, not summit declarations. EDIP capital committed vs announced. SAFE first disbursements achieved but FCAS at existential decision point.

P2_energy_independence.note: Russian gas exit nearing completion (law in force); storage at 29% post-winter (critical); US LNG lock-in growing; hydrogen deployment far behind target.

P3_digital_sovereignty.note: DSA enforcement under US pressure. Digital Omnibus risks diluting AI Act/GDPR. Score enforcement action taken, not regulatory text published.

P4_financial_instruments.note: Lag effects 2+ years. CMU progress. SAFE loans structural positive.

P5_diplomatic_capability.note: Hungary/Slovakia bilateral deviations. Ukraine accession blocked by HU veto. Georgia effectively halted.

Defence industrial base.key_metric: EDIP work programme adopted; SAFE first disbursements; EDF €1.07B awarded. EDIP contracts reaching execution phase.
Defence industrial base.blocker: FCAS IP dispute; MGCS 2045 delay; SAFE conditions and sovereign content requirements

Energy independence.key_metric: Russian gas law in force; LNG capacity +76 bcm. But storage 29% — refill season at risk.
Energy independence.blocker: Middle East LNG competition; US LNG lock-in; hydrogen deployment collapse

Digital sovereignty.key_metric: AI Act prohibitions active; Data Act applicable. But cloud 70% US; Digital Omnibus dilution risk.
Digital sovereignty.blocker: US diplomatic pressure on GDPR/AI Act; no EU hyperscaler

Financial instruments.key_metric: CBAM live; SAFE launched. But US Section 301 investigations target EU; US steel tariffs.
Financial instruments.blocker: Trade coercion from US; QMV blocking on fiscal union

Diplomatic capability.key_metric: Council conclusions on hybrid threats; Ukraine summit Cyprus Apr 23.
Diplomatic capability.blocker: Hungary veto; Georgia accession halted; Serbia dual-track

us_vector_status.note: Shifted from passive dependency to active coercive pressure. Multi-vector simultaneous pressure = strategic squeeze.

score_triggers: First EU common procurement of major platform

calibration_log.summary: US vector calibration, Lagrange 2026 scoring guidance, ReArm Europe scoring discipline, member state tracker upgrades

No domain score movements this week. Hybrid threat incidents updated (HTI-003 EU Council, HTI-004 arson study, HTI-005 airspace). Cross-monitor flags updated (CMF-007 SCEM, CMF-008 FCW, CMF-009 AGM). Hungary election CRITICAL window.

Full ESA-spec module taxonomy baseline built: capability_domains (10 domains), capability_programmes (4), capability_stocks (6), dependencies matrix (8 entries), partner_taxonomy (7 groupings), strategic_compass_milestones (8), trade_instruments (4), enlargement_cfsp (4 country/region entries). 60+ evidence entries created. All existing EGHTM-heritage modules preserved verbatim. lagrange_scoring_2026 and calibration_log updated.
